title: frasera japonica
intro: Frasera japonica, also known as Japanese goldenseal, is a perennial plant species in the gentian family. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea, and skin conditions, such as eczema, and has also been used to lower blood sugar levels and improve immune function. Frasera japonica can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures, allowing for a range of health benefits to be experienced.
